name: The Barony of Letnev
identity: Space vampires with the galaxy's most powerful military aristocracy, commanding through overwhelming firepower and massive fleets.
abilities: Have a bigger fleet than normally is allowed. Can reroll combat dice by spending trade goods. Start with incredible 6-resource production - the most in the game.
playstyle: Aggressive military conquest through superior numbers and firepower. Build massive dreadnought fleets and crush enemies through decisive battles.
fun: You're the faction everyone fears. Your fleets are bigger, your ships hit harder, and you have resources to replace losses. Perfect for players who love rolling dice and winning through raw power.
assets: Begin with advanced technologies (Plasma Scoring + Antimass Deflectors) and enough resources to research 2 more technologies in round 1 - something no other faction can match.

name: Federation of Sol
identity: Humanity's space-faring federation, representing versatility and superior ground forces through advanced military deployment.
abilities: Get an extra action every round. Instantly deploy 2 infantry to any planet you control, and your soldiers are stronger than normal infantry. Your flagship generates free troops automatically.
playstyle: Aggressive expansion with strong territorial control. Excel at taking and holding planets through superior troop deployment and tactical flexibility.
fun: Great for players who don't want one rigid strategy. The extra actions help you learn the game without running out of moves, and you can adapt to almost any situation.
assets: Good balanced starting position with Neural Motivator and Antimass Deflectors technologies, plus 5 infantry for strong ground presence.
weaknesses: Abilities are powerful but not flashy

name: Universities of Jol-Nar
identity: Brilliant aquatic academics who pursue knowledge above warfare - the galaxy's premier researchers and technology developers.
abilities: When others research technology, you can also research without paying a token. You can skip requirements when researching non-military tech. Your ships generate trade goods when enemies move near them.
playstyle: Economic and technological superiority. Focus on collecting technologies and using superior science to compete despite weaker military.
fun: You're the galaxy's mad scientist! Collecting technologies feels rewarding, and out-teching opponents is incredibly satisfying. Great for players who prefer thinking over fighting.
assets: Begin with 2 technologies (E-Res Siphons + Neural Motivator) and 4 commodities for strong early trading position.

name: L1Z1X Mindnet
identity: Ancient cybernetic beings who merged with technology to survive, now seeking to assimilate the galaxy through technological superiority.
abilities: Capture infrastructure when conquering planets instead of rebuilding it. Bombard a planet every combat round instead of only once at the start. Your dreadnoughts can transport extra units.
playstyle: Military conquest with technological advancement. Build feared dreadnought fleets and systematically conquer the galaxy while growing stronger.
fun: Straightforward "build dreadnoughts, conquer planets, get stronger" strategy. Every battle is feared by your opponents, and your robot army theme is very satisfying.
assets: Begin with a dreadnought, strong starting technologies (Plasma Scoring + Sarween Tools), and solid production base.

name: Xxcha Kingdom
identity: Peaceful turtle-like diplomats who prefer negotiation to warfare, serving as the galaxy's mediators and peacekeepers.
abilities: Peacefully claim empty planets adjacent to your territory using diplomacy. Cancel political agendas and force new votes. Strong defensive capabilities.
playstyle: Defensive diplomacy focused on politics and negotiation. Build strong defensive positions while winning through political maneuvering rather than combat.
fun: Perfect for players who enjoy political intrigue over warfare. You're the galaxy's wise peacekeeper, forming alliances and manipulating outcomes through sheer political power.
assets: Strong starting fleet with defensive technology (Graviton Laser System) and good balanced resource base.

name: Yin Brotherhood
identity: Fanatical religious clones who spread their faith across the galaxy, converting enemies to their cause through zealous devotion.
abilities: Convert enemy infantry to join your side during ground combat. Sacrifice your own ships during space battles to guarantee hits on enemies.
playstyle: Aggressive ground-focused combat with conversion tactics. Love invasions where you can turn enemy armies to your faith through religious fervor.
fun: Converting enemies feels incredibly satisfying - nothing beats turning their army against them. The sacrifice mechanic creates exciting tactical decisions.
assets: Balanced fleet with 4 fighters and strong starting technology (Sarween Tools) for good early production.

name: Yssaril Tribes
identity: Chameleon-like espionage masters who manipulate galactic politics from the shadows through superior intelligence networks.
abilities: Skip your turn by discarding action cards to control timing. Draw extra action cards for better selection. Hold unlimited action cards (normally limited to 7).
playstyle: Political manipulation and timing control. Play the long game through careful action card management and strategic positioning.
fun: You're the puppet master pulling strings behind the scenes. The unlimited cards create unique opportunities, and controlling timing keeps opponents guessing.
assets: Neural Motivator technology for card advantage and balanced starting fleet for steady expansion.

name: Emirates of Hacan
identity: Lion-like master traders who built their civilization on commerce, serving as the galaxy's bankers and deal-makers.
abilities: Trade with any player regardless of proximity (others can only trade with neighbors). Refresh commodities for free when trade happens. Start with 6 commodities instead of 3-4.
playstyle: Economic diplomacy focused on profitable deals and negotiation. Be the galaxy's central bank while building economic advantages.
fun: Perfect for social players who love making deals. Every turn involves interesting negotiations, and you're often the kingmaker who tips power balances.
assets: Strong starting technologies (Antimass Deflectors + Sarween Tools) and maximum commodity production for immediate trading advantage.

name: Clan of Saar
identity: Nomadic scavengers who turned displacement into strength, living among asteroid fields with completely mobile civilization.
abilities: Get free trade goods when claiming new planets. Can win even if enemies take your home system. Your space docks can move like ships and produce in the same turn.
playstyle: Mobile opportunistic expansion. Constantly relocate your civilization, swooping in to claim resources then moving when threatened.
fun: You play completely differently from static empires. The mobile civilization concept is unique and creates fascinating tactical decisions about positioning.
assets: Floating space dock and strong carrier fleet for immediate mobile expansion, plus Antimass Deflectors for movement advantage.

name: Naalu Collective
identity: Precognitive snake-people who see the future and manipulate time, always staying one step ahead of enemies.
abilities: Always go first every round. Escape from bad fights to adjacent empty systems. Have superior fighters than can even join in ground combat if your flagship is there.
playstyle: Hit-and-run tactics with superior positioning. Use speed advantage and foresight to strike where enemies are weak while avoiding bad fights.
fun: You feel like a chess master who sees future moves. Always acting first lets you react to everyone's plans, and escaping danger feels cleverly thematic.
assets: Sarween Tools, Neural motivator and a good balance of starting ships.

name: Sardakk N'orr
identity: Giant warrior insects who live for honorable battle, representing the classic proud warrior race archetype.
abilities: +1 to ALL combat rolls in both space and ground battles - they simply fight better than anyone else in every situation.
playstyle: Aggressive military conquest through superior combat effectiveness. Fight early and often, using combat bonuses to win battles others would lose.
fun: You're the galaxy's apex predators. Every fight gives you better odds, making risky battles winnable. Perfect for players who love direct confrontation.
assets: Large starting army (2 carriers, 5 infantry) built purely for invasion and ground combat dominance.

name: The Winnu
identity: Ambitious inheritors of the ancient Lazax Empire who consider themselves rightful rulers, focused on reclaiming the galactic throne.
abilities: Don't pay influence to take the galactic center initially. Get free space dock and planetary defense system when successfully capturing the galactic center.
playstyle: All-or-nothing imperial focus. Rush to control the galactic center quickly and hold the galaxy's political center through determination.
fun: High-risk, high-reward gameplay as the underdog reclaiming their throne. Every game becomes a dramatic race against time with immense tension.
assets: Can choose any starting technology, limited selection of ships and only 2 infantry.
weaknesses: Entirely dependent on galactic center control

name: The Arborec
identity: Living tree entities who grow their forces organically rather than building industrially, representing nature's slow but inexorable expansion.
abilities: Can't build infantry normally - instead get 1 free infantry per round on any planet. Infantry can produce new units, something no other faction can. Infantry can move between nearby planets without ships.
playstyle: Defensive economic growth that snowballs into overwhelming numbers. Start weak but become unstoppable through biological multiplication.
fun: Watching small seedlings grow into a galactic forest is incredibly satisfying. Very unique gameplay focused on biological growth rather than industrial production.
assets: Nice and easy to defend home system. Starts with Magen Defense Grid for planetary defense.

name: Embers of Muaat
identity: Beings of living flame who master stellar destruction, beginning the game with the galaxy's only War Sun superweapon.
abilities: Spend command tokens to create fighters or destroyers in systems with your War Suns. Can fly through (not stop in) supernovas while others cannot. Start with Prototype War Sun.
playstyle: High-risk/high-reward centered on leveraging early War Sun advantage while racing to upgrade it for mobility.
fun: You begin with a planet-killing superweapon! The psychological impact and raw power creates epic moments perfect for players wanting immediate galactic threat status.
assets: Prototype War Sun (slower but devastating), Plasma Scoring technology, but limited early mobility requiring careful positioning.
weaknesses: Vulnerable if War Sun is destroyed

name: Ghosts of Creuss
identity: Mysterious energy beings from another dimension who travel through wormholes, appearing where enemies least expect them.
abilities: All common wormholes types act as one type of wormhole for you. Start outside main galaxy with special wormhole connection. Your flagship is a wormhole, and during the game you can even create new wormholes.
playstyle: Highly mobile hit-and-run tactics through wormhole manipulation. Strike unexpectedly where enemies are weak, then vanish to safety.
fun: Perfect for players who love spatial puzzles and surprising opponents. Your unique movement creates gameplay no other faction can replicate.
assets: Gravity Drive technology and special wormhole tokens, plus fleet positioned outside main galaxy for unique strategic positioning.

name: Mentak Coalition
identity: Former political prisoners who formed their own nation, representing cunning opportunism and making the best of bad situations.
abilities: Get free shots before space combat with cruisers/destroyers. Steal trade goods when neighbors get wealthy.
playstyle: Opportunistic disruption focused on profiting from others' success while using superior early combat capabilities.
fun: You're are the knife. When people get too powerful you cut them down. Every trade is now a 3-way interaction, where you can negotiate if you will steal their resources or not.
assets: Strong combat technologies (Sarween Tools + Plasma Scoring) and balanced fleet optimized for piracy.
weaknesses: People hate you if you steal too much

name: The Nekro Virus
identity: Self-replicating machines that consume and copy everything they encounter, representing the fear of technological assimilation.
abilities: Steal technologies by destroying enemy units in combat. Can't research normally - instead get extra actions. Can copy faction abilities through special technologies.
playstyle: Aggressive expansion focused on constant combat to steal technologies. Must fight continuously to grow stronger through assimilation.
fun: You're the terrifying villain that becomes stronger through every victory! Turning enemies' own tech against them is extremely satisfying.
assets: Strong military fleet including dreadnought for early combat advantage.
weaknesses: Can't vote politically, complex tech management

name: The Argent Flight
identity: Elite starfighter pilots who value honor and tactical excellence, commanding through superior coordination and carrier-based warfare.
abilities: Always have to vote first, but have extra voting power. Use Anti-Fighter Barrage to damage ships even without enemy fighters.
playstyle: Political dominance through superior voting power combined with tactical fighter swarms. Strike Wing Alpha destroyers are uniquely powerful and mobile.
fun: You control galactic politics through voting superiority while commanding elite fighter squadrons. The combination of political leverage and tactical flexibility creates multiple paths to victory.
assets: Strong starting fleet with carriers, Strike Wing Alpha destroyers, and Neural Motivator technology for action card advantage.

name: The Empyrean
identity: Nomadic mystics who fragment across the galaxy, spreading their consciousness through mobile temples that serve as portable home systems.
abilities: Other players can move through your systems if you will so. Ignore nebula movement penalties. Your promissory notes also benefit you instead of being negative.
playstyle: Flexible map manipulation through frontier token adjacency. Control space by facilitating movement for diplomatic leverage while maintaining personal mobility.
fun: Gain political influence by offering passage rights while executing your own strategic movements. Your technology allows you to monopolize frontier tokens.
assets: Good starting resources, Dark Energy Tap technology for frontier exploration.

name: The Mahact Gene-Sorcerers
identity: Ancient genetic manipulators who control enemies through enslaved commanders, turning other factions' leaders into their puppets.
abilities: Capture one command token from each opponent defeated in combat. Use the captured player's commander abilities while holding their token. Gain more fleet capacity while holding captured tokens.
playstyle: Gotta catch them all! Acquire everyone's command tokens to become unstoppable.
fun: You're the puppet master holding your enemies' command tokens. Each captured leader adds new strategic options, growing stronger as opponents activate systems.
assets: Strong home system, Bio-Stims technology for command token flexibility, and mechanisms to exploit captured tokens for advantage.
strengths: Starlancer mech can spend opponent tokens
weaknesses: Dependent on defeating opponents in combat

name: The Naaz-Rokha Alliance
identity: Ingenious explorers and recyclers who turn exploration rewards into powerful upgrades, thriving in resource-poor systems.
abilities: Draw two exploration cards when exploring a planet with a mech, choose one to resolve. Convert relic fragments into relics or command tokens. Mechs can flip into combat form during space battles.
playstyle: Aggressive exploration and relic gathering. Excel in hazardous and low-resource systems, converting gathered relics into strategic advantage through technology research.
fun: You profit from exploration more than anyone else. Your Z-Grav mechs transform into combat-capable ships while retaining mech status, creating unique tactical options.
assets: Psychoarchaeology and AI Development Algorithm technologies, special explorer mechs that transform into combat forms, and relic-focused economy.

name: The Nomad
identity: Time travelling trader and opportunist who controls galactic agendas, profiting from prediction others while staying mobile and unpredictable themselves.
abilities: Start with your flagship that can actually be upgraded. Become rich by predicting agenda outcomes. You have three agents for additional tactical flexibility.
playstyle: Political manipulation and mobile warfare.
fun: You're the mysterious wanderer benefiting from political prediction and transactions everywhere. Your flagship is the only one in the game that can become stronger.
assets: Flagship, Sling Relay technology for movement, extra leader cards for more agent abilities, and strong starting economy.
weaknesses: Your flagship is your powerhouse, but you can only have one.

name: The Titans of Ul
identity: Ancient planetary entities who terraform worlds and create specialized defensive structures, becoming increasingly powerful through PDS networks.
abilities: When upgraded your cruisers become something to be feared. Convert sleeper tokens in activated systems into PDS. PDS units participate in ground combat unlike normal PDS and gain additional combat shots when upgraded.
playstyle: Defensive infrastructure with multi-turn activation sequences. Create an impenetrable fortress of upgraded PDS while converting planets into defensive strongholds.
fun: You're building a planetary empire of impenetrable defenses. Your PDS networks become combat zones for invaders while your terraformed planets grow stronger.
assets: Scanlink Drone Network and Antimass Deflectors, Ouranos flagship deployable from PDS, Hecatoncheires mech that replaces PDS placement.

name: The Vuil'raith Cabal
identity: Parasitic dimensional beings who consume enemy units in combat, growing stronger by capturing opponents' forces and leaving devastation behind.
abilities: Capture non-structure units destroyed in combat (space and ground). Return captured units to produce that unit type without resources. Return captured units to research upgrades ignoring prerequisites.
playstyle: Aggressive combat-focused conquest. Profit from warfare through unit capture, converting enemy losses into your production and technological advantage.
fun: You're the terrifying alien invaders benefiting from every unit destroyed. Enemy losses fuel your production and technology, making combat losses profitable.
assets: Decent fleet with Self-assembly Routines as starting technology, which allows your to easily gain mechs.
weaknesses: Lack of tempo when not fighting

name: Council Keleres
identity: United peacekeeping council formed from the Xxcha, Argent, and Mentak, combining their strengths to maintain galactic order.
abilities: Choose one of three founding factions (Mentak, Xxcha, or Argent Flight) at setup, gaining that faction's home system and corresponding hero. Automatically replenish commodities each round. Spend influence to treat all agenda laws as blank.
playstyle: Adaptive gameplay based on sub-faction choice. Control the political phase through law suspension while building wealth through guaranteed commodity refresh.
fun: You customize your faction at game start by choosing a founding faction's identity. Your guaranteed commodity economy and law control make you a political powerhouse.
assets: Home system varies by chosen founding faction (Mentak, Xxcha, or Argent Flight), plus guaranteed technologies and the Council Patronage commodity engine.
weaknesses: Limited expansion space based on faction choice

name: Last Bastion
identity: Joint military expedition between Xxcha and Sol who galvanize their units, making each soldier fight like ten through sheer determination.
abilities: Galvanize units to roll extra dice in combat and for abilities. Can sacrifice units for guaranteed hits. Produce ships in remote systems through breakthroughs.
playstyle: Quality over quantity warfare. Make every unit count by galvanizing them, turning small forces into elite squads that punch far above their weight.
fun: You're the underdog who never gives up. Galvanizing mechanics make every fight feel heroic, and opponents can never discount your forces.
assets: Balanced fleet with Sarween Tools and Antimass Deflectors, plus built-in abilities to enhance your units' effectiveness.

name: The Deepwrought Scholarate
identity: Renegade Jol-Nar scientists who share knowledge freely, establishing coexistence on planets while building underwater research stations.
abilities: Coexist peacefully on planets with other players while gaining ocean cards. Gain trade goods from ocean cards. Help others research technology in exchange for benefits.
playstyle: Technology-focused cooperation. Spread across the galaxy through coexistence while building a technological and economic advantage through shared research.
fun: You can peacefully coexist on enemy planets! The ocean cards and technology sharing create unique diplomatic opportunities and friendly gameplay.
assets: Good starting technologies and special ocean cards that function as additional planets for resource and influence generation.

name: The Crimson Rebellion
identity: Freed Creuss thralls who travel through breaches instead of wormholes, appearing anywhere on the board through dimensional rifts.
abilities: Place breach tokens that make systems adjacent. Cannot use wormholes. Replace destroyed ships instantly at home. Swap ship positions anywhere on the board.
playstyle: Highly mobile hit-and-run warfare. Create breach networks across the galaxy for rapid deployment while replacing losses through dimensional technology.
fun: You play 4D chess with the game board! Breaches create mind-bending tactical opportunities, and your spatial manipulation keeps enemies guessing.
assets: Unique destroyer fleet that places breaches, plus technologies for ship replacement and starting breach tokens.

name: The Ral Nel Consortium
identity: Business-minded aliens with incredible mobility, transporting structures through space and using speed to outmaneuver all opponents.
abilities: Transport PDS and space docks on ships. Move ships when enemies activate your systems. Unpass to take additional actions after passing. Generate extra action cards.
playstyle: Ultra-mobile warfare with portable infrastructure. Stay unpredictable through survival instincts and unmatched destroyer speed while collecting action cards.
fun: Your stuff moves with you! Transporting structures creates amazing tactical flexibility, and being able to unpass keeps opponents on their toes.
assets: Fast destroyers with movement 3, technologies for mobility, and breakthrough allowing extra action cards.

name: The Firmament / The Obsidian
identity: Populist movement corrupted by a dark blade, transforming from idealists into puppet masters who control opponents through sinister plot cards.
abilities: Score others' secret objectives to place plot cards. Transform into the Obsidian mid-game. Puppet players through plot cards that drain their resources and copy abilities.
playstyle: Two-phase gameplay: subtle manipulation as Firmament, then transformation into the Obsidian for devastating puppeteering. Every player becomes your tool.
fun: You're playing two factions in one! The Firmament-to-Obsidian transformation is dramatic, and puppeting opponents through plot cards is incredibly satisfying.
assets: Dual faction sheets with completely different abilities. Technologies and leaders that transform when you become the Obsidian.
